subject: getEvents race condition on Kafka consumer
commit_message:
getEvents race condition on Kafka consumer

Synchronized access to kConsumetSync() that seems to cause a ConcurrentModificationException.

Issue-ID: DMAAP-1513
Signed-off-by: Julien Fontaine <julien.fontaine@bell.ca>
Change-Id: Ia273153ee8689bfd2874b40dc05a7632efbdd1d3

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dmaap-messagerouter-msgrtr-master-verify-java/306/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/dmaap-messagerouter-msgrtr-master-verify-java/306

Patch Set 3: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dmaap-messagerouter-msgrtr-master-verify-java/307/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/dmaap-messagerouter-msgrtr-master-verify-java/307

Patch Set 3:

Message Router is designed to keep the Kafka Consumer objects in memory to avoid the consumer rebalance. Message Router clients are expected to periodically
read the messages in a topic to avoid the consumer rebalance.  Allowing the coccurrent requests and holding them(by consumer synchronization) until the lock released on the Kafka Consumer object may result in longer response times and also need more changes then in this commit. When the message router pods are scaled up ,it may even lead to many more issues. 

I prefer not to change the existing behavior. If concurrent request processing is expected to handled w/o any errors in the service, proper way is to lock the consumer  and return an error code for the concurrent requests (Similar to locking the consumer using zookeeper to avoid the concurrent request processing(same topic name, consumer group and consumer id) by more then 1 message router pods)

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Message Router is designed to keep the Kafka Consumer objects in memory to avoid the consumer rebalance. Message Router clients are expected to periodically
> read the messages in a topic to avoid the consumer rebalance.  Allowing the coccurrent requests and holding them(by consumer synchronization) until the lock released on the Kafka Consumer object may result in longer response times and also need more changes then in this commit. When the message router pods are scaled up ,it may even lead to many more issues. 
> 
> I prefer not to change the existing behavior. If concurrent request processing is expected to handled w/o any errors in the service, proper way is to lock the consumer  and return an error code for the concurrent requests (Similar to locking the consumer using zookeeper to avoid the concurrent request processing(same topic name, consumer group and consumer id) by more then 1 message router pods)

We're getting ConcurrentModificationException and according to our users they don't send concurrent request but this issue happened. The only way i was able to reproduce this issue was by sending concurrent request. When i synchronized the access to kConsumer.commitSync() i was not able to reproduce anymore. This exception is raised because a thread with a different id tried to access a consumer instance that was already locked. Do you have any idea/guidance about what could cause such behaviour other than an unsynchronized access to the kafka consumer instance ?
I believe the commitSync() should be synchronized.
I removed my other changes.

Allowing the coccurrent requests and holding them(by consumer synchronization) until the lock released on the Kafka Consumer object may result in longer response times and also need more changes then in this commit. When the message router pods are scaled up ,it may even lead to many more issues.

If concurrent request processing is expected to handled w/o any errors in the service, proper way is to lock the consumer  and return an error code for the concurrent requests (Similar to locking the consumer using zookeeper to avoid the concurrent request processing(same topic name, consumer group and consumer id) by more then 1 message router pods)
> 
> We're getting ConcurrentModificationException and according to our users they don't send concurrent request but this issue happened. The only way i was able to reproduce this issue was by sending concurrent request. When i synchronized the access to kConsumer.commitSync() i was not able to reproduce anymore. This exception is raised because a thread with a different id tried to access a consumer instance that was already locked. Do you have any idea/guidance about what could cause such behaviour other than an unsynchronized access to the kafka consumer instance ?
> I believe the commitSync() should be synchronized.
> I removed my other changes.

Sometimes even though the MR don't receive the parallel requests, MR will log the ConcurrentModificationException. This happens in the scenarios when the connection/response from the Kafka took unacceptable time. In this scenario, MR will deliberately use the same Kafka consumer object to create the ConcurrentModificationException and end the request processing. If you see this error very often, it means there are some connectivity issues or Kafka took more time then usual for responding
